key_points: Severe overenrollment forced doubles into triples campus-wide
Housing shortage sent students to Oakland and London campuses
City costs make off-campus living extremely expensive
key_quotes: NEU has a horrid housing crisis and predatory admissions.
Our campus is bursting at the seams. Its not about academics or rankings, we literally overenrolled.
1000 more students than last year... singles are going to be converted to doubles and doubles to triples.

key_points: Greek life is primary social vehicle; tough without it
Co-op rotations constantly scatter your friend group across cities
Everyone cycling in and out makes sustained friendships harder
key_quotes: Social life can mean having to join a fraternity or sorority.
Everyone is moving away for co-op/study abroad/living off campus.
I have completed a co-op and ran my business through each summer — I feel like I've never given myself time to breathe since I got to NEU.

key_points: Mice spotted in Curry Student Center on camera
Students nostalgic for old Pizza Hut and Burger King; current options criticized
Wollaston's on-campus market notorious for overpriced groceries
key_quotes: Curry mouse caught in 4k.
I practically lived on Pizza Hut and Burger King food back in those days.
Wollaston's Market has announced it will be raising its prices 'dramatically and unapologetically'.

key_points: Co-op job market stress is severe, especially CS students
Burnout is real; one student hospitalized with mono from overwork
Administration vague and slow during campus safety incidents
key_quotes: The mental health of students here is extremely bad, worse than those that are angry that they got deferred.
My mental and physical health got bombed last semester from anxiety, insomnia and mononucleosis.
Berklee School of Music students got warning even though all the incidents are right near NEU Boston campus and NEU just adopted 'ignorance is bliss' approach.

key_points: Northeastern Promise covers full demonstrated need for qualifying students
Middle-class families often get minimal aid despite high sticker price
At $80k+ per year, cost is a dealbreaker without substantial aid
key_quotes: The northeastern education is NOT worth $81k/year.
My favorite was Carnegie Mellon sending me $97 loan... Northeastern gave similarly laughable aid to middle-class families.
NEU is overpriced. But tbh their main issue is housing/overenrollment.

key_points: Humanities and liberal arts majors get significantly fewer resources than STEM
Professors are generally strong; some legendary, some terrible
AI arms race between students and professors inflating workloads unfairly
key_quotes: Engineering and business are devoted more resources than the liberal arts majors.
Some profs were terrible and only once a bad prof was canned — only bc rich parent donors complained to the provost.
Every single class has had a CRAZY amount of work compared to previous semesters... professors made assignments longer to compensate for AI usage, hurting anti-AI students.

key_points: Administration routinely ignores student emails and in-person inquiries
Communications during crises are vague corporate boilerplate
Alumni outreach now being written by ChatGPT, students noticed
key_quotes: When I try to come in person no one is there, when I email you no one answers — an internship needs one letter from you just so I can get paid.
'The situation is now under control' — deliberately poor communication from the university.
Does anyone read this stuff? I swear these 'admin' jobs are just free money nepo hires.

key_points: Campus is genuinely beautiful and in a prime Boston location
Underground tunnels between buildings are a major winter lifesaver
MassCass neighborhood nearby has serious safety and drug issues
key_quotes: Campus is beautiful and in a prime location in Boston.
The tunnels are so under hyped. I had 10 mins to go from ISEC to Dodge and the tunnels helped a lot in winter!
That area really sucks. The drug use in that intersection is awful.

key_points: Co-op is genuinely transformative — many students land jobs via it
CS co-op placement collapsed from 90% to 30% in College of Sciences 2025
International students benefit most; co-op provides structured US work experience
key_quotes: The college of sciences went from a 90% co-op placement rate in 2024 to 30% in 2025. They capped the number of co-op applications and still did not place most students.
I did my first co-op in 2019 and have been with the same company since and love my job.
As an international student in the US job market right now… companies literally expect to hire us. I have friends at other schools fighting for random internships with barely any structure.

key_points: Co-op ROI is real but only if you actually land placements
Without strong aid, $80k/year is extremely hard to justify vs. flagships
Students choosing UMD, UIUC over NEU for full rides is common and rational
key_quotes: I came to the conclusion that NEU is not worth the cost offered to me.
Do you think NEU is worth it with 0 aid? Parents will pay all of it — it's still going to be a big financial hit.
UIUC: Full Ride + 10K Stipend vs. Northeastern: 6K in loans every year — the choice seems obvious.

key_points: Hustler culture — driven, career-focused students who don't slow down
Genuine school pride coexists with self-aware jokes about ranking games
Transient feel on campus due to co-ops; community harder to build
key_quotes: NEU isn't and never was a second BU. Northeastern was essentially a commuter college — its history is in educating people who were already working.
Northeastern is extremely competitive whether you're admitted directly to Boston or not.
It's definitely not the right school for every student, but it's the absolute perfect school for me.

key_points: Admissions: deliberately inflates applicant pool with fee waivers, then mass-defers to manipulate rankings
Housing: chronic overenrollment crisis; students assigned to overflow or satellite campuses without warning
Co-op: placement rates quietly collapsing — College of Sciences dropped from 90% to 30% in one year
key_quotes: Got rid of supplemental questions and started giving out fee waivers to get more people to apply then rejected a ton to lower their acceptance rate.
NEU has issues with the nearby communities. We've gentrified them to hell and back as we've expanded.
They made it sound like they placed most of the students in their information sessions and they don't.

key_points: Underground tunnel system connects major buildings — total winter game-changer
NUin study abroad semester abroad is beloved by most who do it
RateMyHusky student-built tool combines RMP and TRACE professor ratings in one place
key_quotes: NUin was literally the best time of my life. Everyone's jealous as fuck when you're back in Boston sharing stories.
All facts! The tunnels are so under hyped.
We built RateMyHusky — RateMyProfessors and TRACE are great but separate. We combined both into one place.
